title: Compress instruction prose

tasks.title: Capture dirty-scope baseline and pre-compression contract-token snapshot
tasks.verification: Baseline artifacts created in /tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse and verified with test -s; no repository changes made by this task.
tasks.implementation_summary: Captured 15-file dirty scope and 2010 token-bearing lines before further edits. Snapshot command/pattern is embedded in the executed Python script and output artifacts in /tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse. adv_task_checkpoint recorded commit f84df1b for the baseline state.
tasks.notes: Created baseline artifacts under /tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse: dirty-files-pre.txt, scope-files-pre.json, contract-tokens-pre.json, baseline-summary-pre.json. Verification command passed. Checkpoint commit f84df1b recorded existing dirty instruction edits as implementation baseline.

tasks.title: Finish caveman-full label normalization
tasks.verification: Stale-label grep passed excluding historical paths; Caveman-full heading/test expectations present.
tasks.implementation_summary: Updated docs/command-voice-standard.md line 496 from `Caveman-lite matches global config` to `Caveman-full matches global config`, preserving surrounding handoff example tokens. Verified no active non-archive `caveman-lite|caveman-light` matches and confirmed `Caveman-full composition` expectations in docs/test. Checkpoint commit 4e66813.
tasks.notes: Replaced the remaining active `Caveman-lite` example clause in docs/command-voice-standard.md with `Caveman-full`. Stale-label grep passed with historical paths excluded; asset-test strings are present.

tasks.title: Update prose-load inventory for this compression pass
tasks.verification: Inventory header updated to POST-COMPRESSION ARCHIVE — pass 3; Pass 3 Delta table for compressInstructionProse/T7 present and verified by rg.
tasks.implementation_summary: Changed inventory lifecycle to `POST-COMPRESSION ARCHIVE — pass 3`, extended the pass list to include T7, and added `Pass 3 Delta: compressInstructionProse (T7)` with rows covering active touched instruction/test surfaces. Verification rg found header, pass section, and T7 rows. Checkpoint commit 4e59aa1.
tasks.notes: Updated docs/prose-load-inventory.md for pass 3/T7 and verified the archive header + delta table anchors.

tasks.title: Audit and complete obvious-win compression across active instruction assets
tasks.verification: Compression audit report created at /tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse/compression-audit-report.json; git diff --quiet confirmed no additional edits needed after baseline/label/inventory tasks.
tasks.implementation_summary: Audited active instruction surface inventory: 82 files, with 15 baseline-changed files. Report written to /tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse/compression-audit-report.json. Confirmed no uncommitted diffs remained for this audit task; no extra edits were safer than the existing baseline compression under C5.
tasks.notes: Generated compression audit report covering 82 active instruction/test surfaces. Existing obvious-win compression was already captured in the baseline checkpoint; no additional safe compression edits identified after label and inventory tasks.

tasks.title: Run stale-label and contract-token verification
tasks.verification: Stale-label grep passed; post snapshot and token diff report exist; token diff report unexpected_diff_count=0.
tasks.implementation_summary: Generated `/tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse/contract-tokens-post.json`, `scope-files-post.json`, and `token-diff-report.json`. Active-surface case-insensitive grep for `caveman-lite|caveman-light` passed with historical paths excluded. Token-diff report: 16 post files, 2268 token-bearing lines, unexpected_diff_count=0, added_files_after_pre_snapshot=[docs/prose-load-inventory.md].
tasks.notes: Stale-label gate passed; post token snapshot and token-diff report created. Token diff report shows unexpected_diff_count=0; docs/prose-load-inventory.md is the only file added after pre-snapshot.

tasks.title: Run focused asset/drift checks and full plugin check
tasks.verification: Focused Vitest passed (2 files, 71 tests). pnpm run check passed. Worktree clean.
tasks.implementation_summary: Ran `pnpm exec vitest run src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` successfully (71 tests). Ran `pnpm run check` successfully (typecheck, isolation/lockfile scripts, lint, format:check). Confirmed final git status clean. Checkpoint recorded at 4e59aa1.
tasks.notes: Focused asset/drift tests passed: 2 files, 71 tests. `pnpm run check` passed. Final worktree status clean.

wisdom.content: For label-normalization checks, use case-insensitive stale-label grep. A prior case-sensitive search missed `Caveman-lite` in an active docs example even though lowercase `caveman-lite` was gone.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal via inline Tier A reply: "continue". Proposal includes problem statement, scope, must-not constraints, success criteria, discovery agenda, and B/F/S scan PASS.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery completed after user AC approval reply `approve`. Agreement persisted and ChangeContract minted with 25 items. Discovery checklist PASS; no ambiguity findings; no spec deltas required.

design.approval_evidence: Design recorded. Mandatory independent validator returned VALIDATED with no conflicts, no user-value tradeoff, and no contract-compromise risk. Auto-continue to planning/prep per design workflow.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep plan with reply `approve`. Six-task graph created with dependencies, TDD intent, and contract refs. Ready task: tk-8a3cad9c50ba.

execution.approval_evidence: All six execution tasks are done. Verification passed: stale-label grep, token snapshots/diff with unexpected_diff_count=0, inventory pass 3/T7 check, focused Vitest (2 files, 71 tests), and `pnpm run check`. Worktree clean at 4e59aa1.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted acceptance summary with reply `accept`. Review verdict APPROVED after remediation; contract matrix persisted with 25 rows and 0 failing; executive summary persisted; tests passed (focused Vitest 71 tests, pnpm run check, stale-label grep).

items.text: Active ADV instruction surfaces use `caveman-full` as the current normative style label where a caveman label is needed.
Audited active instruction assets have obvious compression wins applied without reducing protocol clarity.
Behavioral contracts remain unchanged.
Verification is machine-backed, not visual-only.
Active ADV instruction surfaces no longer define current style as `caveman-lite` or `caveman-light`; normative wording uses `caveman-full`.
All active audited instruction files are reviewed; segments with obvious compression benefit are compressed without reducing protocol clarity.
Contract tokens remain intact: tool names, gates, statuses, slash commands, enums, quoted errors, `MUST`, `NEVER`, approval checkpoints, cancellation approval, archive sign-off, JSON/code examples.
No runtime behavior, schema, or command semantics change.
A fresh prose-load inventory pass records changed/reclassified sections and returns archive status at completion.
Pre/post contract-token snapshots exist for in-scope files; diff is empty or intentional standard-name/test changes are justified.
Focused asset/drift checks and `pnpm run check` pass from `plugin/`.
Prose-only compression except stale standard reference changes and matching tests.
Preserve required command frontmatter shape.
Keep safety, destructive-action, cancellation, and archive-signoff language unambiguous.
Existing prose-load enforcement-class templates remain the governing compression framework.
Compress obvious wins; do not pursue perfect optimization at the cost of protocol clarity.
Do not delete contract-critical instructions only to reduce line count.
Do not alter workflow sequencing, gate ownership, approvals, cancellation approval, or archive sign-off.
Do not edit ADV external state files directly.
Do not treat `caveman-full` as a replacement for prose-load enforcement classes.
Do not rewrite archives/changelog history unless a mention is part of an active instruction surface.
Runtime behavior changes.
Tool schema, enum, gate ID, status, or command semantic changes.
Broad documentation rewrite outside audited instruction surfaces.
User-facing product feature changes.

rows.evidence: Active normative label is `caveman-full`; stale-label grep `! rg -ni "caveman-lite|caveman-light" --glob '!CHANGELOG.md' --glob '!.adv/**' --glob '!docs/archive/**' .` returned no matches.
Compression audit covered 82 active instruction/test surfaces; docs/prose-load-inventory.md pass 3/T7 records changed surface groups; review approved after remediation.
Token snapshots/diff report unexpected_diff_count=0; targeted re-verification resolved restored `MUST` and guard wording findings in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md:904/929 and AGENTS.md:89.
rows.notes: Contract-token loss findings fixed and re-verified.

rows.evidence: Diff is markdown instruction assets plus one asset-test label update; no runtime source, schema, enum, gate, or tool behavior changes.
Case-insensitive stale-label grep excluding CHANGELOG, .adv, and docs/archive returned no output; `caveman-full` anchors present in docs/test.

Audit report at /tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse/compression-audit-report.json covers active instruction assets; Architecture & Quality re-verification resolved all compression clarity findings.
Token diff report unexpected_diff_count=0; re-verification resolved `MUST` restoration, `Do NOT delete worktree`, and worktree guard ambiguity findings.
rows.notes: Contract-token preservation proved by snapshot plus targeted review.

rows.evidence: Git diff contains prose-only instruction/doc updates and matching asset-test label change; focused tests and full check passed.
docs/prose-load-inventory.md lifecycle updated to POST-COMPRESSION ARCHIVE — pass 3 with `Pass 3 Delta: compressInstructionProse (T7)` rows.
Pre/post snapshots exist under /tmp/opencode/compressInstructionProse (`contract-tokens-pre.json`, `contract-tokens-post.json`); `token-diff-report.json` shows unexpected_diff_count=0.
`pnpm exec vitest run src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` passed (2 files, 71 tests). `pnpm run check` passed.
Changes are prose/instruction compression plus stale-label asset-test alignment; no implementation behavior changed.
Focused manifest/doc drift tests passed; command frontmatter shape retained.
Safety/destructive language re-verified: `MUST use as workdir`, `MUST return zero matches`, `Do NOT delete worktree`, and explicit worktree opt-out wording present.
`caveman-full` remains wording-density layer over existing prose-load enforcement templates; inventory and command-voice docs retain framework references.
Only clear compression/clarity fixes were applied; review remediation restored clarity where compression was too aggressive.
Review found and fixed contract-critical wording losses; no contract-critical instruction deleted for line-count reduction.
No workflow sequencing, gate ownership, approval, cancellation, or archive sign-off semantics changed; targeted review found no unresolved issues.
ADV state was accessed only via ADV MCP tools; repo edits were confined to worktree files and /tmp evidence artifacts.
Docs explicitly preserve prose-load enforcement classes as governing framework; `caveman-full` is only wording-density compression.
Stale-label grep excluded historical archive/changelog paths; active instruction surfaces updated only.
Runtime behavior changes were out of scope and not performed.
Tool schemas, enums, gate IDs, statuses, and command semantics were out of scope and not changed.
Broad documentation rewrite outside audited instruction surfaces was out of scope; edits stayed in audited active instruction/test surfaces.
User-facing product feature changes were out of scope and not performed.

acceptanceCriteria: Active ADV instruction surfaces no longer define current style as `caveman-lite` or `caveman-light`; normative wording uses `caveman-full`.
All active audited instruction files are reviewed; segments with obvious compression benefit are compressed without reducing protocol clarity.
Contract tokens remain intact: tool names, gates, statuses, slash commands, enums, quoted errors, `MUST`, `NEVER`, approval checkpoints, cancellation approval, archive sign-off, JSON/code examples.
No runtime behavior, schema, or command semantics change.
A fresh prose-load inventory pass records changed/reclassified sections and returns archive status at completion.
Pre/post contract-token snapshots exist for in-scope files; diff is empty or intentional standard-name/test changes are justified.
Focused asset/drift checks and `pnpm run check` pass from `plugin/`.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal has no Success Criteria section. How will we know this change is done?
Proposal has no Scope section. What files and modules will be affected?
